top_signals.title: ECB Chief Says Inflation Expectations Remain On Target
top_signals.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stressed that long-term inflation expectations are still broadly in line with the 2% target, despite deepening fallout from the Iran war.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: China deepens crackdown on cross-border brokerages
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Frank Elderson: A central banker’s perspective on climate change and nature degradation
Indonesian commodity exporters flag myriad hurdles in state monopoly push
Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
top_signals.summary: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
